issues.main_description_html:
Description

A responsive layout bug was found on the Home / Directory page when viewed on mobile screen widths.

In the photographer directory list cards, the action element on the bottom-right of each card (the "VIEW" / "View Photographer" text/button) is cut off at the right edge of the screen — only a partial "VIE…" is visible. The card content extends beyond the viewport, causing the action to be clipped and the page to scroll horizontally.

This indicates the card layout is not adapting to small viewport widths (the right-hand action column is pushed off-screen instead of wrapping or resizing).

Acceptance Criteria

  • The action text/button on the bottom right of the cards is fully visible and readable on standard mobile screen widths (e.g. 360px to 430px).
  • The UI adjustment does not break or misalign the desktop layout.
  • The cards do not cause unwanted horizontal scrolling on mobile devices.

Scenario Testing (for tester)

Pre-condition: open the Home / Directory page and view the photographer list cards.

# Scenario Steps Expected result
1 Action visible at 360px 1. Open DevTools device toolbar, set width to 360px.
2. Look at the bottom-right "VIEW" action on each card.		 The full action text/button is visible and readable — not clipped to "VIE…".
2 Action visible at 390px / 430px Repeat scenario 1 at 390px and 430px widths. Action fully visible at both widths.
3 No horizontal scroll 1. At 360–430px, try to scroll the page sideways. The page does not scroll horizontally; cards fit within the viewport.
4 Long location text 1. Find a card with a long location (e.g. "GREIFENSEE / MÜLLERWIS / SEILERWIS, SWITZERLAND").
2. View on mobile width.		 Long text truncates/wraps gracefully; the action still stays visible and the card doesn't overflow.
5 Desktop layout unchanged 1. View the directory at desktop width (≥1024px). Card layout/alignment is unchanged from before the fix — nothing misaligned or broken.
6 Tablet / mid widths 1. Check intermediate widths (e.g. 600px, 768px). Layout transitions cleanly between mobile and desktop with no clipping or overflow.
7 Tap target works 1. On a mobile viewport/device, tap the "VIEW" action. Navigates to the photographer profile as expected.

Source: user-reported screenshot (mobile view of the directory cards — "VIEW" action clipped on the right edge).

Demo by Developer

issues.display_title: Issue #12: Handle Issue "View More" Button Renders on Short "About" Descriptions
issues.main_description_html:
Description

A UI logic issue was identified on the Photographer Detail page. In the "About The Photographer" section, the "VIEW MORE" toggle button is currently visible even when the description text is very short (e.g. only 1 or 2 lines).

Showing a "VIEW MORE" button when there is no additional text to expand creates a confusing user experience. The button should be rendered conditionally based on the actual length of the text.

Sub-tasks

  • Implement Conditional Rendering: Update the component logic to check the length of the about text before rendering the "VIEW MORE" button.
  • Set Threshold Logic — handle this in one of two ways:
    • Character Count: Only render the button if text.length > 400 (or an appropriate character limit).
    • CSS Line-Clamp Detection: Use a useRef to detect if the text container is overflowing its CSS line-clamp (e.g. greater than 3 lines). If it doesn't overflow, hide the button.
  • Fix the responsive spacing — it's too close to the right.

Acceptance Criteria

  • If the "About" text is short (e.g. under 3 lines), the "VIEW MORE" button is completely hidden.
  • If the "About" text is long and truncated, the "VIEW MORE" button appears.
  • Clicking "VIEW MORE" successfully expands the text and changes the button to "VIEW LESS" (or hides it).

Scenario Testing (for tester)

Pre-condition: open several Photographer Detail pages with varying "About The Photographer" text lengths.

# Scenario Steps Expected result
1 Short about text 1. Open a profile whose About text is short (1–2 lines / under ~3 lines). The "VIEW MORE" button is completely hidden — no empty toggle.
2 Long about text 1. Open a profile whose About text is long (multiple paragraphs / over the threshold). The About text is truncated and the "VIEW MORE" button appears.
3 Expand toggle 1. On a long-text profile, click "VIEW MORE". The full text expands, and the button changes to "VIEW LESS" (or hides).
4 Collapse toggle 1. After expanding, click "VIEW LESS" (if present). The text collapses back to the truncated state and the button returns to "VIEW MORE".
5 Boundary length 1. Open a profile whose text is right around the threshold (~3 lines / ~400 chars). The button shows only when the text is actually truncated/overflowing — no false toggle when all text already fits.
6 Responsive spacing 1. View the About section on desktop and mobile widths. The "VIEW MORE"/"VIEW LESS" button has proper spacing and is not crowded against the right edge.
7 No layout shift 1. Toggle expand/collapse a few times. Expanding/collapsing is smooth with no broken layout, overlap, or content jump.
8 Empty about 1. Open a profile with no About text (if possible). Neither the truncated text nor the "VIEW MORE" button renders awkwardly (section hidden or clean).

Source: user-reported screenshot (Photographer Detail "About The Photographer" section showing "VIEW MORE" on a short description).

Demo by Developer
